channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Basic Load Balancing for a Web System on DigitalOcean
item.description: In this article we’ll build a simple and inexpensive load-balanced web setup on DigitalOcean. The architecture is intentionally minimal: 1 DigitalOcean Load Balancer 2 small droplets A lightweight...

item.title: I Built a Flight Recorder for AI Agents — Now I Can Replay Every Decision They Made
item.description: 90% of AI agents fail in production. When they do, you get... nothing. No trace, no replay, no step-by-step view of what went wrong. Debugging an agent is like debugging a black box. I built...

item.title: Phishing Forwards: Why Protocol Beats Training
item.description: It happened two weeks after phishing awareness training wrapped up. A well-meaning employee received a suspicious email, wanted to do the right thing, and forwarded it company-wide with a simple...

item.title: The best microSD Express card for the Switch 2 is the cheapest one you can find
item.description: Unlike the original Switch, the Nintendo Switch 2 requires microSD Express cards for storing and playing games. While physically identical to microSD cards (aside from a small, easy-to-miss “EX”...

item.title: Building a Privacy-First Video Converter with WebAssembly
item.description: The Problem Every time you use a typical online video converter, your file gets uploaded to a remote server. For most people that is mildly concerning. For anyone working in healthcare, law, or...

item.title: Minologue: Why I Started Building This Boring AI Application
item.description: A few months ago, I started thinking seriously about moving beyond frontend development. For most of my career, I had been working as a frontend developer — building interfaces, polishing UI...

item.title: I Put a 5MB Rust Binary Between My Code and Every LLM API — It Cut My Bill by 40%
item.description: Every developer using LLMs faces the same three problems: Cost blindness — you cannot answer "how much did I spend today?" No failover — when OpenAI goes down, your app goes down Wasted money —...

item.title: Why it takes you and an elephant the same amount of time to poop
item.description: Article URL: https://www.pbs.org/newshour/health/takes-elephant-amount-time-poop Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47280550 Points: 6 # Comments: 1

item.title: The Trump administration says it can’t process tariff refunds because of computer problems
item.description: The US Customs and Border Protection says it currently can't comply with an order to process billions of dollars in refunds stemming from tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. In a filing on...

item.title: How to Publish Your Browser Extension on the Chrome Web Store
item.description: If you’ve ever built a Chrome extension, getting it in front of users is the next big step. The Chrome Web Store makes this simple, giving you access to millions of users worldwide. Here’s a...

item.title: Archival Intelligence: A Forensic Rare Book Auditor

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

item.title: Feds take notice of iOS vulnerabilities exploited under mysterious circumstances
item.description: The long, strange trip of a large assembly of advanced iOS exploits.

item.title: Nintendo is suing the US government for a refund of Trump’s illegal tariffs
item.description: Nintendo of America is suing the US government over President Trump's tariffs and is demanding a "prompt refund, with interest" of any duties that it has paid, according to a complaint filed in...

item.title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
item.description: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct...

item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now
item.description: It’s 2026, and that means Apple sells MacBooks equipped with not just its capable M-series processors but A-series chips that were previously exclusive to the iPhone. With the MacBook Neo starting...

item.title: The AI Doc is an overwrought hype piece for doomers and accelerationists alike
item.description: We are in the thick of a massive push to incorporate generative AI into almost every aspect of our lives, but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
item.description: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

item.title: Conversational LLM Evaluations in Minutes with NVIDIA NeMo Evaluator Agent Skills
Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous...

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon, the SaaSpocalypse, and why competitions is good, actually
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous...

item.title: How our open-source AI model SpeciesNet is helping to promote wildlife conservation
item.description: Photos of animals being identified by the SpeciesNet AI model

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.description: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is in and Anthropic is out as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the...

item.title: You can already save up to $50 on the new M4 iPad Air
item.description: In case you missed it, Apple announced a host of new devices this week, including both a new phone and a cheaper laptop. It also unveiled the M4-powered iPad Air, which is already receiving a...

item.title: Transparent Tribe Uses AI to Mass-Produce Malware Implants in Campaign Targeting India
item.description: The Pakistan-aligned threat actor known as Transparent Tribe has become the latest hacking group to embrace artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding tools to strike targets with various...

item.title: The Trump phone was a no-show at the world’s biggest mobile show
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This time, we tried, and failed, to find it at...

item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your...

item.title: Multi-Stage VOID#GEIST Malware Delivering XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a multi-stage malware campaign that uses batch scripts as a pathway to deliver various encrypted remote access trojan (RATs) payloads that...
